name: tenzir

metadata.name: tenzir
metadata.summary: A security telemetry engine for detection and response

documentation:
# Tenzir Python

The Python package of Tenzir provides a flexible control plane to integrate Tenzir
with other security tools.

> **Note**
> The Python effort is still highly experimental and subject to rapid change.
> Please do not consider it for production use.

## Usage

To get started, clone the Tenzir repository and install the Python package via
[Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/docs/):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir.git
cd tenzir/python
poetry install -E module
```

## Development

We recommend that you work with an editable installation, which is the default
for `poetry install`.

### Unit Tests

Run the unit tests via pytest:

```bash
poetry run pytest
```

### Integration Tests

Run the integrations tests via Docker Compose and pytest:

```bash
./docker-poetry-run.sh pytest -v
```

## Packaging

The following instructions concern maintainers who want to publish the Python
package to PyPI.

> **Note**
> Our releasing scripts and CI run these steps automatically. You do not need to
> intervene anywhere. The instructions below merely document the steps taken.

### Bump the version

Prior to releasing a new version, bump the version, e.g.:

```bash
poetry version 2.3.1
```

This updates the `pyproject.toml` file.

### Publish to Test PyPI

1. Add a Test PyPi repository:

   ```bash
   poetry config repositories.test-pypi https://test.pypi.org/legacy/
   ```

2. Get the token from <https://test.pypi.org/manage/account/token/>.

3. Store the token:

  ```bash
  poetry config pypi-token.test-pypi pypi-XXXXXXXX
  ```

4. Publish:
  
   ```bash
   poetry publish --build -r test-pypi
   ```

### Publish to PyPI

1. Get the token from <https://pypi.org/manage/account/token/>.

2. Store the token:

  ```bash
  poetry config pypi-token.pypi pypi-XXXXXXXX
  ```

3. Publish

   ```bash
   poetry publish --build
   ```

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.cli.CLI

methods.name: tenzir.tenzir.cli.CLI.__getattr__
documentation: Chains every unknown method call to the internal call stack.
A command-line wrapper for the tenzir-ctl executable.

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.TableSlice
documentation: The Tenzir abstraction wrapping Arrow record batches

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.PyArrowTableSlice
documentation: A TableSlice internally represented by a PyArrow RecordBatch

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir._LazyReader
documentation:
Helper class that instantiates the PyArrow RecordBatchStreamReader in a
dedicated thread to avoid blocking

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.AsyncRecordBatchStreamReader
documentation:
A thread based wrapper that makes PyArrow RecordBatchStreamReader
async

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.Tenzir

methods.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.Tenzir.export
documentation: Executes a query and receives results as Arrow Tables.

methods.name: tenzir.tenzir.tenzir.Tenzir.status
documentation:
Executes the `tenzir-ctl status` command and return the response string.

If `timeout` is greater than 0, the invocation of `tenzir-ctl status` will be
retried every `retry_delay` seconds for at most `timeout` seconds.

Examples: `status()`, `status(timeout=30, detailed=True)`.
An instance of a Tenzir node.

functions.name: tenzir.tenzir.convert.to_pyarrow
documentation: Represent the provided TableSlice as a PyArrow RecordBatch

functions.name: tenzir.tenzir.convert.collect_pyarrow
documentation:
Iterate through the TableSlice stream and sort the record batches by
schema as lists of PyArrow Tables with identical schemas

classes.name: tenzir.tenzir.convert.VastRow
documentation:
A row wise representation of the data and metadata

- name: the Tenzir type for the row
- data: the event data contained in the row

functions.name: tenzir.utils.logging.get
documentation:
Get a logger instance while ensuring that the Tenzir logger namespace
(loggers with name "tenzir or "tenzir.*") is properly configured

modules.name: tenzir.utils.asyncio
documentation:
This module provides asyncio utilities.

These asyncio utilities are based on Lynn Root's blog post series on asyncio at
<https://www.roguelynn.com/words/asyncio-we-did-it-wrong/>. In particular, they
help with gracefully shutting down and proper exception handling to terminate an
application, e.g., after catching SIGINT and SIGTERM.

Example:

: The function `run_forever` is the primary entry point that sets up the
  asyncio event loop and registers exception and shutdown handlers. It takes
  the "main function" task as argument:

  > async def snooze():
  > : for i in range(42):
  >
  >   : await asyncio.sleep(1)
  >
  > run_forever(snooze())

functions.name: tenzir.utils.asyncio.shutdown
documentation: Cleanup tasks tied to the service's shutdown.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.names
documentation: Return all tenzir type names contained in the schema.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.name
documentation: Return the schema name.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.pack_ip
documentation: Convert an ip address to arrow array bytes.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.unpack_ip
documentation: Construct an ip address from a slice of bytes.
Accepts a 128-bit buffer holding an IPv6 address and returns an IPv4 or IPv6
address.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.pack_subnet
documentation: Convert a subnet to arrow array bytes.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.py_dict_to_arrow_dict
documentation:
Build an Arrow dictionary array from a Python dictionary.

- Values should be greater or equal to 0.
- Non-consecutive values will interpolated with null.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.extension_array_scalar
documentation: Create a `pyarrow.Array` from a Python object with extension type support.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.infer_type
documentation:
Map a python type to the corresponding Arrow type.

Note that the arrow -> python mapping is not completely static,
for example timestamp[ns] is converted to `pandas.Timestamp` if pandas
is installed or `datetime.datetime` otherwise. Therefore this function
can also only give a best-effort estimate of the reverse mapping.
TODO @tobim: Consider changing that by implementing our own `to_pydict()`
             alternative.

functions.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.make_record_batch
documentation:
Create a record batch from a python object.

The type of all value must have a corresponding type in the Tenzir type
system.

classes.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.Timestamp
documentation: Create dummy classes so we can safely test
`isinstance(x, Timestamp)` below.

methods.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.SubnetType.__arrow_ext_serialize__
documentation: Explain how to serialize the type metadata.

classes.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.EnumScalar
documentation: Adapter for Tenzir enumeration values.

classes.name: tenzir.utils.arrow.EnumType
documentation:
An extension class for enum data types.

`EnumType` stores as name-to-value mapping called `fields` that maps to the
underlying integer type of represented the enum.

modules.name: tenzir.tools.python_operator_executor
documentation: User code wrapper of the Tenzir python operator.

functions.name: tenzir.tools.python_operator_executor.wrap_recursive
documentation:
Wrap a nested structure of fields.

e.g. {"foo": [1,2,3]} -> _DictWrapper("foo": _ListWrapper([1,2,3]))
The wrappers track if their elements were modified.

functions.name: tenzir.tools.python_operator_executor.unflatten_batch
documentation: Reverts the unflattening performed by _flatten_batch()`.

functions.name: tenzir.tools.python_operator_executor.main
documentation:
Run the user provided code on behalf of the operator.

Expects two open file descriptor numbers as arguments.
The first is for receiving the user code.
The second is to write back a message in case an error occurred.

classes.name: tenzir.tools.python_operator_executor.ResultsBuffer
documentation:
Stores the values produced by the user code.

Builds a new record batch from the all the scalars with the `finish`
function.
